name: West African Groundnut Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound ripe tomatoes, peeled and chopped
1 pound carrots (or a mixture of carrots, turnips, and to make up 1 lb)
piece of grated fresh root ginger or 1 head dried ginger
recipeInstructions:
Cook all ingredients on top of the stove in a very heavy saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, as the meat needs to cook in its own juices. Alternatively, use a pressure cooker as an ordinary saucepan, which seals in steam beautifully. If unsure about the fit of your casserole or saucepan lid, line it with double sheets of foil for added security.
1. Remove seeds from chilies and chop finely.
2. Combine chopped chilies with peanut butter, ginger, herbs, vegetables, and tomatoes. Mix well.
3. Place meat in the pan, followed by the vegetable mixture and some seasoning. Put on a tight-fitting lid and cook very slowly on top of the stove for approximately 1.5 hours or until the meat is tender. Do not worry about apparent lack of liquid, as the meat and vegetables will provide enough.
4. Cook beans for one hour.
5. Cook rice in double its volume of salted water for 15-20 minutes.
6. Season beans with salt and pepper, then mix with rice. Serve as an accompaniment to the stew.
